description: A Class C artifact with valid consensus may have action_policy.action_allowed=false because authorization is separately governed by a declared external policy source. Status remains CONSENSUS_VALID; the action policy is an independent domain (AG2).

input.claim: this action received the required approvals

action_policy.reason: Authorization is separately governed; the declared external policy source has not granted execution authorization despite consensus.
action_policy.policy_rationale: Outside change window; authorization deferred to next window.

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 invariant AG2: execution authorization MUST remain separately governable. Consensus and action_policy are independent domains: required_quorum=2 is met by eligible attesters (CONSENSUS_VALID), AND the declared external authorization_source ('external_policy:org/security/change_window_v1') has independently denied authorization (action_allowed=false). The artifact remains CONSENSUS_VALID; the policy decision is preserved as auditable metadata per AG3 (authorization_source, policy_decision, policy_rationale). The same consensus could yield different action_policy outcomes under different declared authorization sources without changing consensus status — this is the operational shape of AG2. Paired with class-c-consensus-valid (which also yields CONSENSUS_VALID with action_allowed=false but cites AG1 in its reason): same consensus, different policy contexts, same consensus status.
